questions.question: Why can't a pipelined voice agent ever match Moshi's latency?
options: Pipelines can't run on GPUs

The pipeline SHAPE caps you: VAD, STT, LLM, and TTS each have their own minimum latency, flooring at ~300-500ms — Moshi removes the pipeline entirely (theoretical 160ms = 80ms Mimi frame + 80ms acoustic delay, ~200ms practical on one L4)
Pipeline components can't stream
Moshi uses a faster programming language
questions.explanation: You can tune and parallelize a cascade, but a single model with audio in and audio out — text as optional inner monologue rather than a required stage — halves what best-in-class pipelines achieve.

questions.question: What streams does Moshi's 7B Temporal Transformer process each 80ms step?
options: One mono audio stream only

Three in parallel: the user's incoming Mimi tokens, Moshi's own generated Mimi tokens, and Moshi's text inner monologue — so it can hear while speaking, handle interruptions, and back-channel ("mhm") without breaking its utterance
Video, audio, and subtitles
Separate streams per language
questions.explanation: That's what full-duplex means: both audio directions live in the model simultaneously, both at 12.5 Hz × 8 codebooks.

questions.question: Why does the inner-monologue text stream help an audio-to-audio model?
options: It's only for debugging logs

Without explicit text the model must implicitly learn language inside the acoustic stream — forcing text tokens alongside audio improves semantic coherence, eases swapping the LM head, and yields transcripts for free
Text tokens are faster to generate
It enables subtitles legally required in the EU
questions.explanation: The text stream is essentially Moshi's live transcript of itself — language structure gets an explicit channel instead of hiding in codec tokens.

questions.question: What is the depth transformer's job?
options: Estimating speaker distance from the mic

The 8 Mimi codebooks per frame have inter-codebook dependencies, so a small 2-layer transformer predicts them SEQUENTIALLY within the 80ms frame — the standard factorization for AR codec LMs (also VALL-E, VibeVoice)
Compressing the model for edge devices
Adding reverb to the output
questions.explanation: The temporal transformer handles time; the depth transformer handles the RVQ hierarchy within each step — splitting the two axes keeps both tractable.

questions.question: What is Hibiki, and what did Hibiki-Zero change?
options: A Japanese-only TTS engine

Moshi's architecture trained for streaming speech-to-speech TRANSLATION (source audio in, target-language audio out, chunk-by-chunk); Hibiki-Zero (Feb 2026) drops the need for word-aligned data, using sentence-level pairs + GRPO reinforcement learning for latency — a new language takes ≈1000 hours
A watermark detection model
A music remixing system
questions.explanation: Same full-duplex codec-LM design, different task. The broader Kyutai stack (Kyutai STT, Pocket TTS, Unmute) runs 64 concurrent sessions at 3x realtime on one L40S.
